target.claim: Implemented tier-one OS sandbox backend coverage for script workers
target.separation: This matrix is separate from Postman API parity and from future scripted high-volume execution support.

claimSurfaces.platform-os-sandbox: Implemented native OS sandbox backends and packaged validation wiring across tier-one desktop platforms. Stable production readiness still requires native-runner evidence in the production readiness matrix.
claimSurfaces.postman-api-parity: Postman script API behavior and imported-script observable output.
claimSurfaces.high-volume-scripting: Future scripted high-volume execution contract; currently out of scope.
claimSurfaces.release-gate: Validation plumbing required to keep the matrix current.

statuses.implemented: Implemented and covered by automated validation or source-owned contract checks.
statuses.blocked-native-backend: Reserved for future platform rows that lack a native OS sandbox backend or packaged validation.
statuses.out-of-scope: Explicitly outside the current claim surface.
statuses.validation-hook: Validation plumbing exists and must stay release-gated.

rows.target: Future scripted high-volume execution remains outside the Postman scripting parity claim unless a separate contract is written and implemented.
rows.securityDecision: Do not imply partial scripted high-volume execution compatibility until the future feature has its own sandbox contract.

rows.target: Linux script workers launch through bubblewrap with unshared namespaces, no host network namespace, dropped capabilities, cleared environment, and private writable tmpfs mounts.
rows.securityDecision: Use bubblewrap as the first OS-level backend and require it for Linux production validation.

rows.target: Packaged Linux artifacts must prove the OS sandbox backend, Node permission flags, ASAR/path behavior, and sandbox worker launch still work after packaging.
rows.securityDecision: Keep packaged Linux OS-sandbox validation in CI and release workflows before shipping artifacts.

rows.target: Linux bubblewrap launches install a seccomp cBPF deny policy for high-risk kernel APIs such as bpf, ptrace, keyring, mount, process_vm_*, perf_event_open, io_uring, unshare/setns/clone3, and related nested namespace syscalls.
rows.securityDecision: Keep the dangerous-syscall deny policy in the Linux release gate as the accepted current Linux syscall-policy standard.

rows.target: Maintainer decision: platform-equivalent current Linux coverage does not require a maintained deny-by-default seccomp-BPF allowlist beyond the current bubblewrap namespace isolation plus dangerous-syscall deny policy.
rows.securityDecision: Accept the current Linux bubblewrap plus dangerous-syscall seccomp policy for the current Linux public claim; track a deny-by-default allowlist only as optional future hardening.

rows.target: Packaged macOS artifacts validate the seatbelt backend, Node permission flags, app bundle/ASAR path behavior, private temp behavior, and script-worker execution on a macOS runner.
rows.securityDecision: Keep packaged macOS OS-sandbox validation in CI, manual native release validation, and release workflows before shipping artifacts.

rows.target: macOS script workers launch through the system seatbelt sandbox-exec backend with a deny-default profile, denied network access, explicit process-exec/process-fork denial, no broad process allowance, minimal environment, private writable temp directory, and read-only runtime/app/library access.
rows.securityDecision: Use the platform seatbelt launcher where available and fail closed in required validation if the profile cannot launch or does not enforce the expected boundary.

rows.target: Postman script API parity is tracked by the Postman parity matrix, official-docs coverage audit, and claim gate; platform OS sandbox completion is intentionally not part of npm run postman:parity:claim.
rows.securityDecision: Keep compatibility gaps and platform OS sandbox gaps on separate claim gates so one cannot mask the other.

rows.target: The OS-sandbox platform matrix is generated, committed, validated, and wired into check, CI, release workflow, and release-gate manifest validation.
rows.securityDecision: Require matrix freshness and structure in normal validation, while production readiness remains responsible for moving native-runner/manual evidence from implemented to validated.

rows.target: Windows script workers launch through the release-owned AppContainer helper with a stable AppContainer profile, minimal child environment, private temp directory, no broad filesystem grants, no declared network capabilities, inherited stdio only, and a kill-on-close single-process job object.
rows.securityDecision: Use a release-owned AppContainer helper instead of relying on node:vm alone; source and packaged Windows validation must fail closed if the helper is missing or cannot launch.

rows.target: Packaged Windows artifacts validate the native helper is included as an extra resource and that AppContainer helper launch, Node permission flags, packaged path behavior, and script-worker execution work on a Windows runner.
rows.securityDecision: Keep packaged Windows OS-sandbox validation in CI, manual native release validation, and release workflows before shipping artifacts.
